what_it_does: Declares a module-level AI_TIC dict for 'versioning': creating v2/round-N/dated copies instead of updating the canonical file in place. Carries id, label, markers, and description.
observations: Docstring names the rule 'no-versioning: update the SAME file every pass.'
AI_TIC keys: id ('versioning'), label ('versioning'), markers (9 phrases), desc.
Largest marker set in the batch (9 phrases), covering filename-suffix and prose forms.
standouts: Markers mix filename suffixes ('-final', '_final', '(copy)') with prose version labels ('v2', 'round 2').
conventions: Same seed-as-data dict shape as sibling ai_tics files.
notes: A seed file defining the 'versioning' tic — flagging v2/copy/-final artifacts that violate update-in-place.
questions: Are the suffix markers matched against filenames, file content, or both?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the versioning seed tic exactly as described.
intention_signals: Encodes Tim's update-the-same-file rule as detectable markers so versioned/dated copies can be flagged as machine tells.
novelty: Cataloguing version-suffix vocabulary itself (including filename forms) as a detectable anti-pattern.
contribution: Contributes the 'versioning' tic definition and its v2/copy/-final markers to a fused tic catalogue.
summary_for_embedding: A Python seed file defining the 'versioning' AI-tic dict with markers for v2/round-N/copy/-final versioned artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To detect and subtract versioned/dated copies, enforcing update-the-canonical-file-in-place.
